channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Nibi the 'diva' beaver won't be released, Massachusetts governor decides
item.description: The question of whether a 2-year-old beaver named Nibi can stay with the rescuers she has known since she was a baby or must be released into the wild has been resolved, thanks to the governor of Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli crowds rally across the world on the eve of Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Crowds were participating in pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli protests and memorial events across Europe, North Africa and Asia on Sunday on the eve of the first anniversary of the Hamas attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KREX 5

item.title: Video: Plane catches fire while making hard landing in Las Vegas
item.description: Frontier Flight 1326 landed in Las Vegas after "the pilots detected smoke and declared an emergency," a spokesperson for Frontier Airlines confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: US disaster relief chief blasts false claims about Helene response as a 'truly dangerous narrative'
item.description: The U.S. government’s top disaster relief official says false claims and conspiracy theories about the federal response to Hurricane Helene, which are being spread most prominently by Donald Trump, are “demoralizing” aid workers and creating fear in people who need recovery assistance.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Donald Glover cancels Childish Gambino tour dates after recent surgery
item.description: Donald Glover has canceled the remaining dates of Childish Gambino’s North American and European tour. The musician, actor and “Atlanta” creator announced on Friday that he recently discovered an ailment that required surgery. After his New Orleans show on Sept.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Walz says he'll 'own up' when he misspeaks as the Democratic ticket steps up media interviews
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly a week after verbal stumbles in the only vice presidential debate, Democrat Tim Walz used his debut campaign appearance on a Sunday news show to try to fend off criticism of his stand on abortion rights and “own up” to past misstatements.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Here's what Spirit Halloween seeks in an 'ideal' retail location
item.description: Spirit Halloween made headlines earlier this week after firing back at "SNL" over a sketch that mocked the company's choice of retail locations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Harris, Walz to do several high-profile interviews this week
item.description: Harris' media stops this week will include 60 minutes, Howard Stern The View, the Call Her Daddy podcast and Univision.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Milton is growing stronger as it blows toward Florida's Tampa Bay region
item.description: The National Hurricane Center says Hurricane Milton is intensifying and will likely be a major hurricane before it slams into the storm-ravaged Florida coast, most likely on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Voters in North Carolina and Georgia have bigger problems than politics. Helene changed everything
item.description: Residents in North Carolina’s Watauga County are focused on survival rather than politics after Hurricane Helene ravaged much of the western part of the state.
item.dc:creator: Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump returns to site of attempted assassination, honors shooting victims
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams channels Trump as he ramps up revenge accusations against the Biden admin, expert says
item.description: Two New Yorkers on opposite sides of the political spectrum have a similar beef with the federal government — allegations of politically motivated prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge rules Missouri sex offenders no longer need to post warning signs outside their homes on Halloween
item.description: A federal judge ruled that Missouri's sex offenders are no longer required to display "no candy" warning signs outside their homes on Halloween, arguing it is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohtani homers in playoff debut and the Dodgers rally to beat the Padres 7-5 in NLDS opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani hit a three-run homer in his postseason debut, Teoscar Hernández’s two-run single gave Los Angeles its first lead in a playoff game in two years, and the Dodgers beat the San Diego Padres 7-5 in their NL Division Series opener Saturday.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Keanu Reeves spins out at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in pro auto racing debut
item.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves made his professional auto racing debut on Saturday in an event in which “The Matrix” star spun out at famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Dog alerts family of fire helping them escape
item.description: Officials with the Spearman Volunteer Fire Department released information regarding a mobile home fire that happened Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dailyn Wells, NewsNation

item.title: Frontier flight catches fire while making hard landing in Las Vegas
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Flames and smoke were seen coming from a plane that made a hard landing at Harry Reid International Airport in Las Vegas on Saturday afternoon. Frontier Flight 1326 landed in Las Vegas around 3:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 30-foot wall of water hit Chimney Rock, firefighter recalls; officials refute 'bodies everywhere' rumor
item.description: Chimney Rock volunteer firefighter John Payne saw what he estimated was a 30-foot-high wall of water, tossing car-sized boulders as it raced toward the town.
item.dc:creator: ALLEN G. BREED Associate Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former New York governor and stepson assaulted during evening walk
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. David Paterson and his stepson sustained minor injuries when they were assaulted on a Manhattan street by four men and a woman as they went for an evening walk, police said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Capitol Police officer recounts Jan. 6: 'It's hard to relive'
item.description: Former Capitol Hill Police Officer Winston Pingeon recalled the events of Jan. 6, 2021, when rioters breached the Capitol in an apparent attempt to stop Congress’s certification of the Electoral College results, in an interview with ABC News. "It's hard to relive and talk about moments like this.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tropical Storm Milton expected to reach major hurricane strength before Florida landfall
item.description: Tropical Depression Fourteen has strengthened into Tropical Storm Milton, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Russian prosecutors seek 7-year sentence for US man accused of fighting for Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian prosecutors asked for a seven-year sentence in the trial of a U.S. citizen accused of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine against Russia, Russian news agencies reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A week after Helene hit, thousands still without water struggle to find enough
item.description: Nearly a week after Hurricane Helene brought devastation to western North Carolina, a shiny stainless steel tanker truck in downtown Asheville attracted residents carrying 5-gallon containers, milk jugs and buckets to fill with what has become a desperately scare resource — drinking water.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, JEFF AMY and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang member nabbed in NY 2 years after Border Patrol released him into US
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement arrested a Venezuelan illegal immigrant on gun charges who previously had been twice detained and released by local law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey sisters battle stage 4 colon cancer together
item.description: They share a lot more than their sister bond. They are both in their late 30s and currently battling stage four colon cancer.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, KRON4

item.title: Bernie Sanders shows athleticism but ‘finally’ meets his match in ping pong
item.description: It’s taken 83 years, but Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders has met his match. In ping pong, that is. Video posted to social media by one of Sanders’ advisers shows the senior U.S. senator battling it out with a headband-clad boy in Austin, Texas, earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Air Force cadet's cause of death blamed on rare bacterial infection
item.description: Avery Koonce, a 19-year-old Air Force Academy cadet who was found dead in her dorm room on Sept. 4, died from a rare bacterial infection, a coroner says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: False and misleading claims about Hurricane Helene are spreading about the storm and recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New video shows Kentucky sheriff pointing gun at judge before alleged fatal shooting
item.description: Newly released surveillance footage shows when a Kentucky sheriff pointed his gun at a judge's head before allegedly shooting and killing him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rachel Morin's mom comes face-to-face with illegal immigrant murder suspect for first time in court
item.description: Rachel Morin's family came face-to-face for the first time with her suspected killer, an illegal immigrant who wore an "emotionless expression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Satellite shows before-and-after flooding in North Carolina after Helene
item.description: Planet Labs captured and compared satellite images that show the storm's devastating impact on one of the hardest hit cities.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, WGN 9

item.title: Trump returns to site of attempted assassination, honors shooting victims
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, Fox 5

item.title: Biden: 'I don't know' if Netanyahu trying to influence election
item.description: President Biden on Friday said he wasn't aware of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was trying to influence the U.S. election, while noting no administration has been a stronger ally to Israel than his own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Houston teens carjack driver to smuggle migrants
item.description: Two men have pleaded guilty to a carjacking in South Texas where they said they stole a vehicle in order to smuggle migrants into the country.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5
